# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF, show cycle counts
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \

---
name: FPGA benchmark compile/run approach
description: How to compile and run benchmarks on the Andes FPGA - use simple gcc with -mvh, not picolibc specs
type: feedback
---

For compiling programs to run on the Andes FPGA board:
- Just use the toolchain's built-in newlib: `riscv64-elf-gcc -Os -mvh -o foo.elf foo.c -lm`
- Do NOT use `--specs=picolibc.specs`, `--oslib=semihost`, `--crt0=semihost`, `-Wl,--defsym` hacks, etc.
- `-mvh` enables Andes virtual hosting (semihosting) — required for printf output via GDB
- Toolchain: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- GDB (no-python): `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython`
- The picolibc build (`build_mculib3.sh`) produces `libm.a` etc. for benchmarking, but linking for FPGA runs should use the simple approach above.

When benchmarking picolibc's libm specifically, link against the built picolibc libm.a directly rather than through specs.

---
name: libnn performance counter reference
description: ~/work/libnn uses nds_pfcounter.h with Andes M-mode CSR inhibit/reset/read pattern for cycle profiling
type: reference
---

libnn performance counter infrastructure is in:
- ~/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/include/nds_pfcounter.h

Pattern: _startPFM() inhibits counters (mcountinhibit=0x7F), resets mcycle/minstret to 0, re-enables.
_stopPFM() inhibits, reads counters, checks overflow via Andes CSR_MCOUNTEROVF (0xFC9), re-enables.
Uses __nds__mtsr/__nds__mfsr intrinsics from nds32_intrinsic.h.

Andes-specific CSRs: MMSC_CFG (0xFC2), MCOUNTEROVF (0xFC9).
Cache HPM events: 0x61 (dcache load access), 0x71 (dcache load miss), 0xc1 (dcache stall), 0x91 (dcache store miss).

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc

  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else

ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \

ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \

  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =

%.o: %.s

	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o

define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT

	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \

			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all          — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF via QEMU
#   make bench-sin          — bench a single function via QEMU
#   make fpga-bench-all     — run bench ELFs on FPGA board via GDB
#   make fpga-bench-sin     — FPGA bench a single function
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> fpga-bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run perf() path, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf < /dev/null
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf < /dev/null
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# FPGA bench rules: run bench ELFs on real hardware via GDB/semihosting
define FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_SINGLE
fpga-bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

define FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
fpga-bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

fpga-bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),fpga-bench-$(f)) \
                $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),fpga-bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))

lines: -.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
+.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all fpga-bench-all clean list \

- Added `FPGA_GDB_FLAGS` with the `-batch -ex ...` sequence from your command
- Added `FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_SINGLE` and `FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT` define blocks
- Added `fpga-bench-all` top-level target
- Updated `.PHONY`

Usage:
```sh
make XLEN=64 fpga-bench-sin
make XLEN=64 fpga-bench-all
make XLEN=64 FPGA_REMOTE=other-board:1234 fpga-bench-sin
```

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all          — build, run opt vs ref with EN_PERF via QEMU
#   make bench-sin          — bench a single function via QEMU
#   make fpga-bench-all     — run bench ELFs on FPGA board via GDB
#   make fpga-bench-sin     — FPGA bench a single function
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> fpga-bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_FPGA_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

fpga-bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),fpga-bench-$(f)) \
                $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),fpga-bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all fpga-bench-all clean list \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),fpga-bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),fpga-bench-$(f))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run perf() path, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)

# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))

ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run on FPGA via GDB, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all fpga-bench-all clean list \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),fpga-bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),fpga-bench-$(f))

lines: -.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all fpga-bench-all clean list \
+.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \

content.text: Done. `bench-*` now builds with `-DEN_PERF` and runs on the FPGA via GDB. QEMU and `fpga-bench-*` variants are gone.
